subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: -0.93% | NVDA stop touched — AVGO selloff day 3
html_body:
Daily Portfolio Check

Monday, 8 June 2026 — 11:05 PM Kuwait Time (4:05 PM ET)

Total P&L (vs cost basis)

−$6.59 (-0.93%)

Today

−$20.49 (−2.84%)

🎯 Withdrawal goal: $813.34 / $1,000 — $186.66 to go (81.3% there)

Day 3 of the AVGO-triggered semi selloff. NVDA's stop at $204.00 was touched intraday today ($204.01 low, closed $204.04). This is not a fundamental break — NVDA's Q1 FY27 earnings were a massive beat. Decision needed tonight.

🚨 Decisions needed — before Tuesday 9:30 AM ET open

1. NVDA: Stop $204.00 touched today (intraday low $204.01, close $204.04). Exit or hold with conviction? Call it tonight.

2. LLY: TP1 hit ($1,114). Currently $1,123. Trim 30% (0.042 shares)? Net profit after $3 commission: +$2.41. Decide before open.

Position Snapshot

Status Ticker Close* Day P&L $ P&L %
🚨 ALERT NVDA $204.04 −0.52% −$18.43 −6.14%
🔔 ACTION LLY $1,123.00 −0.74% +$18.03 +12.88%
⚠️ WATCH AVGO $385.00 −8.09% −$8.52 −6.56%
✅ HOLD TSM $406.00 −2.21% +$3.95 +4.54%
⚠️ WATCH AMD $415.00 −9.54% −$1.62 −3.23%
TOTAL — −2.84% −$6.59 −0.93%

*June 8 closing prices estimated from confirmed intraday data. Prior-day closes (June 5, confirmed): NVDA $205.10, LLY $1,131.42, AVGO $418.91, TSM $415.17, AMD $458.80. Verify exact figures in your broker app. Portfolio file last updated: 13 May 2026.

Position Detail

NVDA🚨 ALERT $204.04 (-0.52%)

Stop $204.00 touched today. Intraday low: $204.01 — one cent above the stop. Close: $204.04. The context that matters: NVDA Q1 FY27 earnings (May 20) were a massive beat — revenue $81.6B (+85% YoY), EPS $1.87 (beat vs $1.76 est), Data Center $75.2B (+92% YoY), $80B buyback authorization added. The current weakness is 100% AVGO contagion from the June 3 guidance miss. The thesis is intact. The stop is being challenged by sector sentiment, not by anything NVIDIA did wrong.

Option A — Exit Tuesday open: Gross: 1.3799 × ($204.04 − $217.40) = −$18.43. Commission: −$3.00. Net loss: −$21.43.
Option B — Hold: AVGO's AI revenue grew 143% YoY — the selloff is an expectations miss, not fundamental deterioration. If the sector recovers, NVDA could retrace to $215–220. Risk: if selling continues, next support ~$195 (additional ~$12 per share risk, or 1.3799 × $12 = $16.56 more).

Your stop is your stop. If $204 was set for a reason, respect it. Decide tonight — not at 9:31 AM.

Stop: $204.00 ⚠️ TP1: $240 TP2: $260 TP3: $285
LLY🔔 ACTION $1,123.00 (-0.74%)

TP1 hit. At $1,123 you're $9 above TP1 ($1,114). LLY dropped just 0.74% today while semis bled 2–10% — the non-correlation thesis is working exactly as intended. Orforglipron (oral GLP-1, no injection) Q2 2026 launch is the next catalyst and hasn't fully priced in.

TP1 trim math: Sell 30% = 0.04221 shares at $1,123. Proceeds: $47.40. Cost: 0.04221 × $994.87 = $41.99. Gross: +$5.41. Commission: −$3.00. Net: +$2.41.

Gross exceeds $3 threshold — valid trim by the rules. Net is thin but positive. Case for trimming: locks in gain while semis are chaotic. Case for holding: orforglipron launch catalyst still ahead; TP2 at $1,243 (+10.7% from here). Your call — both are defensible.

Stop: $915 TP1: $1,114 ✓ HIT TP2: $1,243 TP3: $1,393
AVGO⚠️ WATCH $385.00 (-8.09%)

Stop at $379 is 1.56% away. AVGO Q2 FY26 results (June 3): Record revenue $22.2B (+48% YoY), AI semiconductor revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY, above their own forecast), EPS $2.44 (beat vs $2.40 est). The selloff is a whisper-miss: Q3 AI guidance of $16B (up 200% YoY) missed the buyside estimate of $17.2B. CEO Hock Tan did not raise the $100B FY27 AI target. Long-term supply deals with Google, Anthropic, OpenAI, and Meta are intact. Fundamentals are strong; sentiment is broken short-term.

If stop hits at $379: Gross: 0.315 × ($379 − $412.04) = −$10.41. Commission: −$3.00. Net loss: −$13.41.

Do NOT lower the stop to avoid the loss. Let it do its job. Watch pre-market Tuesday for direction.

Stop: $379 ⚠️ 1.6% away TP1: $474 TP2: $536 TP3: —
TSM✅ HOLD $406.00 (-2.21%)

Most resilient semi in the portfolio — still +4.54% above entry ($388.35) despite Friday's −6.69% and today's −2.21%. Stop ($357) provides 12.5% of room from current price. TP1 at $435 not yet in range. The AVGO guidance miss is a chip design company sentiment problem, not a foundry utilization problem — TSMC's wafer demand from NVDA, AMD, and Apple is driven by end-market orders, not Broadcom's Q3 forecast. No action needed. Geopolitical monitor: no Taiwan Strait headline this week.

Stop: $357 TP1: $435 TP2: $485 TP3: —
AMD⚠️ WATCH $415.00 (-9.54%)

Worst single-day move in the portfolio (−9.54%). AMD had run to an all-time high of $542.52 on June 3 — the day of AVGO's earnings — making it the most expensive, most exposed name going into the selloff. Now at $415, it's −3.23% below your entry of $428.84. Stop at $386 provides another 7% before it triggers. Small position size ($48.56 market value) limits the damage. Core risk: next catalyst is Q2 earnings ~August 4, 2026 (57 days away). MI300X hyperscaler traction with MSFT and Meta is real, but without a near-term catalyst the stock can drift lower. No action — let the stop protect you.

Stop: $386 TP1: $493 TP2: $558 TP3: —

💵 Cash deployment — $113.00 available

Reserve $3 min → deployable: $110. Buy commission: $3. Net into position: $107 (above $75 minimum).

Option A — Add to TSM: 0.263 shares at ~$406 = $107. Risk: MODERATE (47/100). Blended entry drops from $388.35 to ~$391. Cheapest valuation in the portfolio (~18x forward P/E), foundry thesis unchanged by AVGO's guidance miss.

Option B — Start MSFT: 0.255 shares at ~$420 = $107. Risk: MODERATE (Tier A halal). Azure AI is the demand-side of your chip thesis — when hyperscalers spend on AI, MSFT Azure revenue grows. Not exposed to chip-hardware guidance risk. MSFT June 5 close: $416.67.

Recommended: Hold cash as dry powder 3–5 trading days. VIX closed 21.51 on Friday (approaching the 25 no-new-positions threshold in the playbook). NVDA and AVGO stops may resolve this week — if both exit, you free ~$395 to redeploy with full clarity post-selloff. A $107 MSFT starter today isn't worth the timing risk while the semi rout is still live.

Your call.

🔍 Intelligence Layer

Top Screener Candidates

MSFT — Valuation gap + financial strength + catalyst (Q4 FY26 earnings late July). Azure AI exposure is the demand-side mirror of your chip thesis, without chip-guidance downside risk. Best portfolio diversifier vs 4/5 semi concentration.
ANET — Momentum + growth + catalyst. AI networking infrastructure is the next spending layer after GPUs. If hyperscaler capex stays elevated (AVGO's actual Q2 results confirm it is), network fabric is the next bottleneck. No exposure to chip design guidance risk.
NVO — Growth + financial strength + pipeline catalyst. GLP-1 duopoly partner to LLY. If you trim LLY at TP1, NVO preserves GLP-1 exposure under a different patent/pipeline profile.

Risk Scores (current holdings)

NVDA | 45/100 | MODERATE | Elite fundamentals; stop breach + 42% portfolio concentration
LLY | 38/100 | MODERATE | Fair GLP-1 valuation; orforglipron launch not yet priced in
AVGO | 48/100 | MODERATE | Thesis intact (AI +143% YoY); stop-proximity is the active risk
TSM | 47/100 | MODERATE | Foundry monopoly intact; geopolitical tail risk binary/low-prob daily
AMD | 56/100 | HIGH | Most elevated valuation; no catalyst until Aug 4 earnings

What to do — Tuesday 9 June

1. NVDA decision by 9:30 AM ET: Exit (net −$21.43) or hold. Not deciding IS a decision to hold — make it consciously before the open.
2. LLY trim (optional): If trimming, sell 0.042 shares at market. Net: +$2.41. Protects gain while semis are volatile.
3. AVGO stop watch: Check pre-market. If semis open red again, be ready for $379 to trigger. Do not move the stop.
4. AMD — no action. Stop at $386 gives room; small position.
5. VIX check: Above 25 → hold all cash. Below 18 and sector stabilizing → revisit MSFT Option B.

📚 Today you learned

Earnings Whisper Numbers

Sellside analysts published Q3 AI revenue consensus at $17.2B for AVGO. Buyside desks — hedge funds running their own channel checks on hyperscaler capex trends — had a higher number in their models. That's the whisper. When Hock Tan guided $16B on June 3, AVGO didn't miss Wall Street. It missed the whisper. That's why the stock dropped 15% on results that were, by any objective measure, exceptional: Q2 AI revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY), EPS beat, record total revenue. The actual print was good. The expected magnitude of outperformance was better — and that gap is what moved the stock. Your NVDA is at its stop today not because of anything NVIDIA did wrong, but because the sector repriced to AVGO's whisper miss. Understanding this doesn't tell you to hold or exit — the stop is the stop — but it explains why strong fundamentals and a bad price can coexist in the same session.

Also today: Sellside vs buyside — sellside analysts (banks, brokers) publish public consensus estimates; buyside (hedge funds, institutions) run proprietary models that drive actual price discovery.

Research output, not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results. All June 8 prices estimated from confirmed intraday ranges — verify exact closing prices in your broker app before placing any orders. Price sources: Yahoo Finance, Investing.com, CNBC, MarketBeat (multi-source cross-check). Confirmed prior-day closes (June 5): NVDA $205.10, LLY $1,131.42 (all-time high close), AVGO $418.91, TSM $415.17, AMD $458.80. MSFT June 5 close: $416.67. VIX June 5 close: 21.51 (CBOE/FRED). NVDA Q1 FY27 earnings source: SEC Form 8-K May 20, 2026 (revenue $81.6B, EPS $1.87). AVGO Q2 FY26 earnings source: CNBC / Motley Fool transcript June 3, 2026. Halal status verified May 11, 2026 via Musaffa (AAOIFI) — re-verify quarterly. Generated 8 Jun 2026, 23:05 Kuwait time (AST).
